emotional_tone: Grounded, premium, and calm. This is a finance app that avoids the cold, clinical feel of standard SaaS. It feels like an expensive, physical ledger.

rules.headings: Tracking tight, uppercase labels with wide tracking, stark contrast.
rules.body: Leading relaxed, highly legible for data.

layout.strategy: The Control Room Grid (Dense but clear)
layout.spacing: Generous spacing philosophy. Use p-6, p-8 for containers. Massive gaps between sections (gap-12).
layout.borders: Grid Borders: Expose the skeleton. Use 1px sharp borders border-border/40 instead of floating cards.
layout.radius: rounded-none or max rounded-sm (2px). Sharp edges emphasize precision.

surfaces.cards: Flat background, 1px subtle border, no shadow. Hover: slight lift (-translate-y-1) and subtle shadow.
surfaces.header: Solid background with a hard bottom border. No glassmorphism.
surfaces.badges: Sharp, rectangular or very subtle rounded-sm. Tinted backgrounds with high-contrast text.

motion.entrance: Staggered fade-in and slide-up (translate-y-4) for dashboard cards and table rows.

images.description: Subtle warm paper texture for an optional header background layer or empty states. (Use with very low opacity, like 5-10%)

icons.instruction: Use duotone or regular weight. Phosphor adds a highly refined, premium tech feel.

components.StatCards: Rectangular, sharp edges. Use a label size (text-xs uppercase tracking-widest) for the title, and a huge highly tracking-tight number for the value. No background color, just a 1px border. On hover, darken border.
components.RecentInvoices: Grid or list. Remove default card shadows. Use subtle dividers between rows.
components.OutstandingBalance: Highlight this with the primary accent background (#3E5745) and white text to create visual tension and focal point. Or use the subtle paper texture behind it.
components.Table: Minimalist header, 1px bottom border on rows. High row padding (py-4).
components.StatusBadges: Don't use generic rounded-full pills. Use small rectangular tags (rounded-sm) with strong earthy colors (terracotta for overdue, sage for paid, sand for pending).
components.Navigation: Flat, text-based, wide spacing. Active state uses a bold underline or text color change, not a generic gray blob.

testing.data_testids: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g. data-testid='kpi-total-revenue', data-testid='invoice-status-badge').

instructions_to_main_agent: Install `@phosphor-icons/react` for icons via yarn add @phosphor-icons/react.
Include font links in index.html for Cabinet Grotesk and Satoshi. Alternatively, map them to a clean sans-serif stack in Tailwind config if CDN not used.
Remove all drop shadows from cards and panels. Rely on 1px borders (border-collapse style) and spacing to create structure.
For the outstanding balance card, use the dark green primary color with off-white text to make it the hero element of the dashboard.
Convert status badges from bright generic tailwind colors (red-500, green-500) to the earthy hex values provided in the colors section.
Apply `data-testid` to all vital UI components as per critical rules.
Ensure the .js files (not .tsx) are correctly modified. Use JS syntax and preserve existing components structure.
Implement the 'Control Room Grid' by stripping out excessive card aesthetics, enforcing sharp borders (rounded-none or max rounded-sm), and utilizing generous padding.
